title: Enhance mol-pr-triage to produce 3-section morning-briefing format
description:
Parent: dr-* (city tracking bead for end-to-end PR loop). Stephanie's design 2026-05-12.

Edit /home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-pipeline/formulas/mol-pr-triage.formula.toml (or .beads/formulas/ — locate canonical path first; mol-pr-from-issue lives in pr-review/, mol-pr-triage may live in pr-pipeline/) to produce the morning-briefing format below.

Current behavior: produces a triage report listing candidate issues with classifications.

Desired behavior: produce a structured 3-section briefing suitable for daily 7am EDT slack post.

**Section 1 — In flight (N PRs)**

List every open PR Stephanie authored on gastownhall/gascity. For each:
- PR number + title
- State indicator: ✓ CI green / ⏸ iterating / ⚠ stalled / 🔵 awaiting reviewbot
- Last status change (gh pr view + statusCheckRollup)

Query: `gh pr list --repo gastownhall/gascity --author '@me' --state open --json number,title,url,statusCheckRollup,reviews,updatedAt`

**Section 2 — Needs your call (M issues)**

For each triage candidate classified as `needs-decision`:
- Issue number + title + priority
- Classifier flag (which criterion triggered needs-decision: design-doc reference, requires-* label, first-time-contributor-language, etc.)
- File a dr-* bead in city scope with status=open + classifier reasoning
- 1-click commands embedded in the briefing:
  - To proceed with shape A: `bd update <bead> --set-metadata decision=shape-A` + `gc-sling /home/ds/gascity/polecat <bead> --on mol-pr-from-issue --var issue=<N> --var auto_push=true`
  - To defer: `bd update <bead> --status deferred --set-metadata blocked_on=<reason>`
  - To drop: `bd close <bead> --reason "skipping — out of scope"`

The 1-click commands let Stephanie copy-paste a single line to resume automation after her decision.

**Section 3 — Skipped (X issues)**

Triage candidates that didn't qualify even for escalation (assigned to someone else, closed, has open PR via Closes/Fixes, etc.). Brief one-liner per entry.

**Section 4 — Stats**

- Triage candidates surveyed
- Auto-shipped (will be 0 until Phase 3)
- Needs decision count
- Skipped count
- (Future) Auto-shipped success rate last 7d

**Output format:** markdown, suitable for slack publish-to-channel. Header includes timestamp + workspace ("ds-research mayor").

**Output path:** `.gc/pr-pipeline/morning-briefing/YYYYMMDD-HHMM.md` (similar to existing triage report path pattern). Also write `evidence.briefing_path` on close so loop-close handler can find it.

Acceptance:
- Formula TOML parses
- Step body produces the 3+1 section output
- Backwards-compatible: callers using the old format (if any) get a similar shape; the briefing IS the new format

Halt-and-mail-mayor on: ambiguity about which mol-pr-triage canonical path to edit, or if classifier rule semantics need clarification.
notes:
commit: e1e5259
branch: bd-gpk-0bu
build: n/a (formula-only, no compile step)
tests: TOML parse verified via python tomllib
formula_version: 2 (bumped from 1)
step_structure: setup, fetch, classify, report, finalize (5 steps, preserved)
ready_for_push: yes

Notes:
- Canonical path resolved at design time: pr-pipeline/formulas/mol-pr-triage.formula.toml (the .beads/formulas/mol-pr-triage.formula.toml and .beads/formulas/mol-pr-triage.toml entries in the parent repo are symlinks to it; no path ambiguity halt needed).
- Output reshaped to 4-section morning briefing: In flight (Sec 1) / Needs your call (Sec 2) / Skipped (Sec 3) / Stats (Sec 4).
- Path changed: .gc/pr-pipeline/triage/<TS>.md → .gc/pr-pipeline/morning-briefing/<YYYYMMDD-HHMM>.md.
- evidence.briefing_path set on root bead at close.
- Tier 1+2 (grab-now/good-candidate) deliberately NOT surfaced as a section — they flow toward Phase 3 auto-ship; for now reflected in Section 4 stats as 'Auto-shipped (Phase 3): 0' + a Tier1+2 staging count.
- One tracking bead filed per Tier 3 item (in local bd DB; prefix determined at runtime per the local DB config — 'dr-*' in city scope, 'gas-*' or similar in rig scope).
- Halt-and-mail-mayor wired on classifier semantic ambiguity per bead spec.
- Commit e1e5259 also contains the persistent bd-hook leak (issues.jsonl, +65 lines).
close_reason: mol-pr-triage v2 produces 4-section morning briefing, files needs-decision beads with 1-click resume commands, parses cleanly, ready for review + push

title: Edit mol-pr-from-issue: add auto_push var + gate-auto-push-eligibility step
description:
Parent: dr-1l9hc4 (Autonomous polecat end-to-end ship).
Design: /home/ds/gas-city/docs/design/autonomous-pr-ship.md
Spec: dr-1l9hc4 notes "MAYOR JUDGMENT 2026-05-12T09:30Z" section.

Edit /home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-from-issue.formula.toml:

1. Add new var in [vars] block:
```
[vars.auto_push]
description = "Positive signal authorizing polecat push + open-PR after eligibility gate. Default false. Required-positive — absence treated as halt-at-branch-ready regardless of skip_open_pr."
default = "false"
```

2. Insert new step `gate-auto-push-eligibility` between `gate-after-ship` and `open-pr`. The step body checks (in order, each a hard gate):
   - auto_push=true (else skip gate; halt at open-pr via existing skip_open_pr early-exit)
   - diff-size ≤ 500 LOC (git diff origin/main...HEAD --numstat, sum added+removed)
   - diff-files ≤ 8
   - path-protection: no paths matching `.github/**`, `cmd/gc/dispatch_runtime.go`, `cmd/gc/session_lifecycle_*.go`, `internal/api/**`, `internal/config/**`, `**/secrets/**`, `hooks/**`, `.beads/**`, `**/migrations/**`, `bin/gc-*`
   - issue-label: not in {breaking-change, requires-design, requires-discussion, epic, tracking, arch, discussion-needed, policy}
   - issue-state: OPEN, no existing open PR referencing via Fixes/Closes #N
   - base-ci: no fresh main CI failure within 6h
   - commit-count: ≤ 5 commits ahead of origin/main
   - branch-name: matches ^(fix|feat)/.+-[0-9]+$
   - polecat-confidence: must equal "high" (read from earlier step's metadata polecat_confidence; default medium)
   - kill-switch: /home/ds/.gc/auto-push-armed.flag must exist; if absent, write evidence.review_required=true + halt with summary_for_human routed to mayor

3. On gate failure: bd update root_id --set-metadata gc.halt_chain=true gc.escalate_to=mayor summary_for_human="Auto-push eligibility halt for issue #N: <gate>: <detail>"; bd close root_id --reason "auto-push gate halt: <gate>"

4. On gate pass: write evidence.gate_passed=true; set GASCITY_SHIP_BYPASS=auto_push_validated:<root_id> in environment for the open-pr step.

5. Edit open-pr step: at top, check evidence.gate_passed=true AND env GASCITY_SHIP_BYPASS set. If not, halt with summary_for_human. If yes, proceed through existing push + gh pr create.

Acceptance:
- Formula TOML parses (toml-cli or equivalent)
- No regressions on existing skip_open_pr=true path (still halts at branch-ready)
- auto_push=false default preserves existing behavior end-to-end
- All gate checks are mechanical (no LLM judgment); polecat-confidence is the only soft input
- Run `make build` clean in /home/ds/gascity-main if any go side artifacts (probably none — formula is TOML only)

Halt-and-mail-mayor on: any ambiguity in spec, any case where the gate logic needs LLM judgment.
notes:
commit: 24475abc47a4e5ad3160d4b467dd46b6be5627ea
branch: bd-gpk-fdq
build: n/a (TOML only — no go-side artifacts per bead acceptance)
tests: pass (python3 tomllib parse OK; 7 steps; DAG: pr-start → gate-after-pr-start → ship → gate-after-ship → gate-auto-push-eligibility → open-pr → drain; auto_push in [vars])
ready_for_push: yes, but with caveat — see below.

Deliverable
-----------
pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-from-issue.formula.toml:
- New [vars.auto_push] (default 'false').
- New step gate-auto-push-eligibility inserted between gate-after-ship and open-pr. Implements all 10 mechanical gates per dr-1l9hc4 design: kill-switch (/home/ds/.gc/auto-push-armed.flag), diff-size <=500 LOC, diff-files <=8, path-protection (.github/, cmd/gc/dispatch_runtime.go, cmd/gc/session_lifecycle_*.go, internal/api/, internal/config/, **/secrets/, hooks/, .beads/, **/migrations/, bin/gc-*), issue-label exclusion list, issue-state OPEN + no existing Fixes/Closes PR, base-ci no fresh main failure within 6h, commit-count <=5 ahead, branch-name ^(fix|feat)/.+-[0-9]+$, polecat-confidence == 'high'.
- Skip path when auto_push != 'true': step is a no-op; chain falls through to open-pr soft-halt. No halt_chain set, no evidence written.
- Halt path on any gate fail: gc.halt_chain=true, gc.escalate_to=mayor, summary_for_human names the failed gate, bd close.
- Pass path: evidence.gate_passed=true + evidence.auto_push_bypass_token + export GASCITY_SHIP_BYPASS=auto_push_validated:$ROOT_ID for the gascity-ship-gate PreToolUse hook.
- open-pr step: needs updated to ['gate-auto-push-eligibility']; step 1 reads GATE_PASSED and BYPASS_TOKEN; new step 2.5 soft-halts at branch-ready when GATE_PASSED != 'true' (mirroring skip_open_pr=true exit, distinct summary), then re-exports GASCITY_SHIP_BYPASS from metadata in case env didn't survive the step boundary.
- skip_open_pr=true early-exit preserved verbatim.

Caveat: env hook contamination
------------------------------
The commit also touches:
- .gitignore (added /issues.jsonl as a defensive entry)
- issues.jsonl (65-line bd export, force-staged by the bd pre-commit hook)

The bd pre-commit hook is configured locally via /home/ds/gascity-packs/.beads/config.yaml ('export.git-add: true') to git-add a fresh root-level issues.jsonl on every commit. It uses git add -f, so /issues.jsonl in .gitignore does NOT block it (I tested). Bypassing the hook with --no-verify or core.hooksPath=/dev/null was off-limits per polecat rules. Prior polecat PRs (#13, #11, #10, #9, #8) somehow shipped without this file, but I could not identify the workflow that scrubbed it without violating hard rules.

Before pushing, strip the export and amend (or just cherry-pick the formula change onto a clean branch):
  git rm --cached issues.jsonl && git commit --amend --no-edit
The .gitignore entry can stay — it's a defensive marker if bd updates the hook to honor .gitignore in the future, and it documents the situation for the next polecat.

Acceptance recap
----------------
- TOML parses: yes (python3 tomllib).
- skip_open_pr=true path: preserved verbatim, still soft-halts at branch-ready.
- auto_push=false default: now soft-halts at branch-ready per the design's required-positive semantics (mol-pr-from-issue is phase = 'vapor' and was added in the same day's commit feaf365/squash 77502dd; this change is consistent with the design's intended end-state, not a regression on a stable prior behavior).
- All gate checks are mechanical (counts, regex, shell exit codes); polecat-confidence is the only soft input, read as a mechanical metadata lookup with default 'medium' treated as gate-fail.
- No go-side artifacts.
close_reason: formula edited per dr-1l9hc4 design; gate + open-pr modifications committed on bd-gpk-fdq @ 24475abc47a4e5ad3160d4b467dd46b6be5627ea; issues.jsonl auto-staged by bd hook needs strip before push

title: gc slack publish-to-channel returns 0 even when adapter receipt has delivered=false
description:
Parent: dr-6vhzid (loop-close gap). Source: gc-esm4 investigation writeup at /home/ds/gas-city/.gc/investigations/gc-esm4-loop-close-tally.md §6 "Latent defect in `gc slack publish-to-channel`'s exit code."

**Problem:** /home/ds/gascity-packs/slack-pack/scripts/slack_chat_publish_to_channel.py returns 0 unconditionally on adapter HTTP-200, regardless of whether the receipt JSON contains `delivered: false`. The PL stamps `loop_close_posted_at` on the bead based on the CLI's exit code — so any future slack rejection (auth fail, channel renamed, scope change, rate limit translated to delivered=false) silently masquerades as a successful post.

Today this is not the symptom (every publish today returned `ok: true` from slack). But it's a ticking-time-bomb defect: when slack DOES reject for any reason, the loop-close handler stamps success and the post is permanently lost (no fallback fires, no error surfaces).

**Fix:** in slack_chat_publish_to_channel.py, after the adapter HTTP-200 path, inspect the receipt JSON. If `receipt.delivered == false` (or any error field is set), `sys.exit(1)` with a clear stderr message. Mirror the same pattern in any sibling scripts that wrap adapter publishes (slack_chat_publish.py, slack_chat_reply_current.py, etc. — audit during the fix).

**Reference adapter behavior:** /home/ds/gascity-packs/slack-pack/adapter/main.go:1140-1203 (handlePublish). The adapter logs `slack POST error:` and `slack returned error:` only when ok=false. The HTTP response body carries `delivered: <bool>` regardless.

**Files to inspect/edit:**
- /home/ds/gascity-packs/slack-pack/scripts/slack_chat_publish_to_channel.py (primary)
- /home/ds/gascity-packs/slack-pack/scripts/slack_chat_publish.py
- /home/ds/gascity-packs/slack-pack/scripts/slack_chat_reply_current.py
- /home/ds/gascity-packs/slack-pack/scripts/slack_intake_common.py:583-635 (publish_to_channel_via_adapter — returns receipt JSON; HTTP-error paths raise correctly per writeup)
- /home/ds/gascity-packs/slack-pack/tests/test_slack_chat_publish.py (extend test coverage)

**Acceptance:**
- CLI scripts exit non-zero when `delivered=false` in adapter receipt
- Stderr message is parseable and includes the failing reason (auth/channel/etc.)
- Existing tests pass; new test covers the delivered=false branch
- No regression on the success path (delivered=true still exits 0)

**Halt-and-mail-mayor on:** adapter behavior changes (e.g., if delivered is renamed or removed in the receipt schema, need to coordinate with adapter.go)

title: Create mol-pr-revert formula for emergency PR revert
description:
Parent: dr-1l9hc4. Answers open question 4 (revert path).

Create /home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-revert.formula.toml.

Contract: revert a merged PR. Mayor-only invocation initially (no PL dispatch row). Uses same scoped carve-out as mol-pr-from-issue: requires auto_push=true + gate verdict pass (or explicit GASCITY_SHIP_BYPASS=mayor_revert).

Vars:
- pr (required, int): PR number to revert
- reason (required, string): explanation for the revert (lands in PR comment + commit message)

Steps:
1. intake-pr — gh pr view N --json mergedAt,mergeCommit,headRefName; assert merged
2. verify-revertable — gh api /repos/.../commits/<merge_sha>/branches-where-head; assert merge_sha is on default branch; check no subsequent commits depend on it via simple history scan
3. revert-branch — git checkout -B revert/pr-<N> origin/main; git revert --no-edit <merge_sha>; git push fork revert/pr-<N>
4. open-revert-pr — gh pr create with title "Revert PR #N: <reason>"; body links to original PR and reason
5. comment-on-original — gh pr comment N with note + revert-PR URL
6. report — write evidence.revert_pr_url + summary_for_human

Default agent: polecat (mayor-invoked, so polecat must accept the push step — uses same auto_push carve-out)
Acceptance: formula parses; push step gated on either auto_push=true OR explicit mayor-set bypass.
notes:
commit: 56ef34609e0eb5767969a5f1ad34415fbbaf5e62
branch: bd-gpk-bnk
build: n/a (formula-only — TOML parse + bd cook dry-run only)
tests: pass (python3 tomllib OK; bd cook --mode=runtime --dry-run resolves all 6 steps with correct needs chain; {{pr}} and {{reason}} substitute in titles)
ready_for_push: yes

artifact: pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-revert.formula.toml (623 lines)

spec deviation: bead spec called for gh api .../branches-where-head to verify merge_sha is on default branch; that endpoint returns branches WHERE the commit IS THE HEAD which is the wrong semantic (default branch moves past the merge commit immediately). title: Create mol-pr-iterate formula for PR feedback iteration
description:
Parent: dr-1l9hc4. Addresses gc-k7x4.

Create /home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-iterate.formula.toml.

Contract: iterate on machine or human feedback for an outgoing PR. Source-agnostic pipeline; source determines input parser only.

Vars:
- pr (required, int): PR number on gastownhall/gascity
- feedback_source (required, enum): codecov | copilot | review-comment | text
- feedback_ref (optional, string): comment id, codecov report url, or inline text

Steps:
1. intake — gh pr view, checkout PR branch, record base SHA on root bead
2. parse-feedback — dispatch on feedback_source:
   - codecov: gh api codecov_report_url; identify uncovered lines in diff
   - copilot: gh api /repos/.../pulls/N/comments filter by author copilot
   - review-comment: gh api /repos/.../pulls/N/comments/<id>
   - text: read feedback_ref directly
   Write structured feedback to root bead notes.
3. propose-patch — polecat reads feedback, proposes minimal diff. Writes plan to .gc/pr-pipeline/iterate/pr-<N>.md
4. apply — implement plan; commit with conventional message; verify build green
5. verify-clearance — re-run feedback source check; for codecov: re-trigger workflow; for copilot/review-comment: post a reply-comment noting the change
6. report — write evidence.* + summary_for_human

Default agent: polecat
Halt-and-mail-mayor on: feedback ambiguity, scope exceeds 200 LOC, or verify-clearance fails after 2 iterations.

Acceptance:
- Formula TOML parses
- Each step has clear contract + exit criteria
- No auto_push integration (this is an outgoing-PR iteration, not a from-scratch PR)
notes:
commit: 06bc222
branch: bd-gpk-lmm
build: n/a (formula-only, no compile step)
tests: TOML parse verified via python tomllib
formula_steps: intake, parse-feedback, propose-patch, apply, verify-clearance, report (6 steps)
ready_for_push: yes

Notes:
- Formula parses cleanly. 6 steps as specified by bead.
- vars: pr (required int), feedback_source (required enum: codecov|copilot|review-comment|text, validated in intake), feedback_ref (default empty, required for review-comment and text — validated in intake)
- Halt-and-mail-mayor: parse-feedback on ambiguity; propose-patch on plan > 200 LOC; verify-clearance on iteration >= 2 still-failing. Pattern uses gc.halt_chain + gc.escalate_to=mayor + summary_for_human metadata AND an explicit 'gc mail send mayor' call (combining mol-pr-from-issue's halt convention with the bead's literal 'mail-mayor' wording).
- Plan artifact path: .gc/pr-pipeline/iterate/pr-<N>.md (matches mol-pr-from-issue's .gc/pr-pipeline/ convention; .gc/ is gitignored).
- No auto_push integration as required.
- verify-clearance has source-specific behavior: copilot/review-comment post a reply comment; codecov verification is deferred-to-push (no way to re-trigger codecov from a local commit); text marks manual-verification.
- Commit 06bc222 also contains the persistent bd-hook leak (issues.jsonl, +65 lines, same pattern as commits 24475ab and f41ba62 and chore-cleaned by 01b9d9f).
close_reason: mol-pr-iterate formula created, parses cleanly, 6 steps (intake/parse-feedback/propose-patch/apply/verify-clearance/report), halt-and-mail-mayor pattern implemented, ready for review + push

title: mol-do-work
description:
Simple work formula — read the bead, do what it says, close it.

This is the minimal work lifecycle for coding agents. No git branching,
no worktree isolation, no refinery handoff. The agent reads the bead's
description, implements the solution in the current working directory,
and closes the bead when done.

Use this for demos and simple single-agent workflows. For production
multi-agent setups with branch isolation and merge review, use
mol-polecat-work instead.

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | caller | The work bead ID assigned to this agent |

title: Read assignment, implement, and close
description:
You have been assigned a work bead. Read it, do the work, and close it.

**1. Read your assignment:**
```bash
bd show gpk-24c
```

Read the bead's title and description carefully. This is your task.

**2. Implement the solution:**

Do exactly what the bead describes. Follow existing codebase conventions.
Make atomic, focused commits as you work:
```bash
git add <files>
git commit -m "<type>: <description>"
```

**3. Close the bead when done:**
```bash
bd update gpk-24c --status=closed --notes "Done: <brief summary of what you did>"
```

If you get stuck or need clarification, check your inbox:
```bash
gc mail inbox
```

**Exit criteria:** The work described in the bead is complete and the bead is closed.

title: Signal completion
description:
Work is done. Signal the controller to reclaim this session:

```bash
gc runtime drain-ack
```

Run this command and nothing else.

title: [dr-01zan7-impl] Add Common-pitfalls section to gascity-packs AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md
description:
# Add "Common pitfalls" section to gascity-packs AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md

## Why

Parent: city bead `dr-01zan7` — full design spec. This bead is the
gascity-packs-side half (2 of 4 files). Sibling bead in gascity rig
(`gc-*`) covers the gascity-side half. Keep the two halves IN SYNC
modulo Claude-vs-Codex flavor differences.

## Files to touch

- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/AGENTS.md` (Codex reads this)
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/CLAUDE.md` (Claude reads this)

## Content to add

Same shape as the gascity-side sibling bead — a new "Common pitfalls
(observed failure modes)" section between Architecture and Development
workflow, with four sub-sections sourced from mayor memory. **Identical
content to the gascity sibling bead's section** modulo flavor — these
two halves need to stay in sync because polecats working on either repo
hit the same failure modes.

### Sub-section 1: Before committing

- ❌ Don't run `bd export` from worktree root — issues.jsonl lands at
  ROOT, not `.beads/`. **gascity-packs worktrees are the MOST affected
  by this** — the bd pre-commit hook in this repo auto-stages
  issues.jsonl at root. `git status -s` + `git diff --cached --stat`
  before every commit. Force-push only scrubs from branch tip;
  dangling commit stays reachable by SHA until GitHub GC. (Source:
  `feedback_bd_hook_leaks_issues_jsonl.md`)
- ❌ Don't stack PRs. Julian's auto-scope-check rejects. Rebase off
  main if the base hasn't merged. (Source:
  `feedback_julian_rejects_stacked_prs.md`)

### Sub-section 2: When opening a PR

- ❌ `gh pr edit --add-label` fails with projectCards GraphQL warning.
  Use `gh api repos/gastownhall/gascity-packs/issues/<N>/labels -X POST -f labels[]=...`
  instead. (Source: `reference_gh_pr_edit_label_workaround.md`)
- ❌ `maintainerCanModify: true` doesn't mean push works — fork perms
  can still 403. On 403, supersede via our fork. (Source:
  `reference_maintainer_can_modify_misleading.md`)
- ❌ PRs from forks need workflow approval. CI silent? Check for
  `action_required` runs and approve. (Source:
  `reference_first_time_contributor_ci_gate.md`)
- ❌ Also check `isDraft: true` — draft PRs can't merge. `gh pr ready`
  first.

### Sub-section 3: When reviewing incoming PRs (adopt-pr)

- ✅ "Take the good" is the default. Request-changes is a last resort.
  Scope-creep or sibling-tangled PRs → surface via rollup, don't
  auto-fire request-changes. (Source:
  `feedback_pr_review_take_the_good_policy.md`)
- ✅ Be kind when superseding contributor work. Thanks + credit + name
  the overlap. (Source: `feedback_kind_to_contributors_when_superseding.md`)
- ✅ Verify issue authorship before retest-commenting upstream.
  (Source: `feedback_verify_issue_authorship.md`)
- ✅ `@copilot review` comment manually triggers reviewbot. (Source:
  `reference_copilot_review_on_demand.md`)

### Sub-section 4: After PR opens

- ✅ Reviewbot greenlight + minor changes → fixup + self-merge
  (Julian's rule). Or label `status/needs-review-auto`. (Source:
  `feedback_self_merge_or_review_auto_label.md`)
- ✅ quad341 (Jim Wordelman) is a peer maintainer. (Source:
  `reference_quad341_is_maintainer.md`)
- ✅ Maintainer scope: gascity + gascity-packs ONLY. Other gastownhall
  repos hit independent triage. (Source:
  `feedback_maintainer_scope_gascity_only.md`)

## Drift check

After editing, spot-diff:
- gascity-packs/AGENTS.md vs gascity-packs/CLAUDE.md (intra-repo)
- gascity-packs/AGENTS.md vs gascity/AGENTS.md (cross-repo, from the
  sibling bead) — pitfalls should match

## Acceptance

- [ ] Both files have the "Common pitfalls" section in the correct
  position
- [ ] All four sub-sections present
- [ ] Each pitfall cites its source memory file inline
- [ ] Drift check passes (intra-repo AND cross-repo against gascity)
- [ ] PR opened against gastownhall/gascity-packs:main

## Coordination with sibling bead

The gascity-side child bead is `<gc-*>` in the gascity rig (filed
simultaneously). Both PRs reference `dr-01zan7` in their body for the
parent design context. Author them simultaneously and cross-link the
two PRs once both are open.

## Mayor memory reference

`/home/ds/.claude-homes/account5/.claude/projects/-home-ds-gas-city/memory/`
— same 12 source files as the gascity sibling.

title: [mol-pr-from-issue] Macro formula: pr-start → ship → open-PR with halt-on-flag gates
description:
# mol-pr-from-issue — macro formula: pr-start → ship → open-PR with halt-on-flag gates

## Why

Stephanie's desired end-to-end flow from a `gascity-triage` result is:

1. She picks an issue from the ranked queue
2. PL slings pr-start for that issue
3. If pr-start raises critical flags → halt + surface in Slack thread
4. Else continue to mol-pr-ship
5. If ship raises major concern → halt + surface in Slack thread
6. Else open the PR (push fork + gh pr create)

Today this is three independent formulas (mol-pr-start, mol-pr-ship,
plus the manual open-PR step) and the PL needs three slack messages
from her to advance through. This bead packages the chain as one
macro formula with explicit gates between steps.

This is the "chain-of-small-steps" pattern from Lexler's catalog: a
multi-step pipeline with explicit per-step gates rather than a single
monolithic formula. Each gate is a halt-point — work pauses, signals,
and waits for clearance.

Parent planning: dr-i06w09 (Lexler patterns map) — this advances the
"chain-of-small-steps" + "feedback-loop" rows.
Phase-2 dependency: dr-1lf4h3 (loop-close PL-mediated rewrite) — the
PL-mediated nudge mechanism is what surfaces flags to Slack at each
gate.

## Formula shape

`/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-from-issue.formula.toml`

```toml
description = "From a triaged issue to an open PR. Runs pr-start → ship → open-PR with halt-on-flag gates between steps."
formula = "mol-pr-from-issue"
phase = "vapor"
version = 1

[vars]
[vars.issue]
description = "The upstream GitHub issue number to author a PR for"
required = true

[vars.skip_open_pr]
description = "Stop at the ship-green / branch-ready state, do not actually open the PR. Default false."
required = false
default = "false"

# Steps run in order. Each step ends with a gate: if any critical-flag
# evidence is present on the step's bead, the chain halts and the
# user is surfaced via dr-1lf4h3 PL-mediated loop-close (handler
# nudges PL, PL posts findings to originating slack thread).

[[steps]]
id = "pr-start"
title = "Plan + scaffold the PR for issue {{issue}}"
# Reuses mol-pr-start step body verbatim (copy-with-prune) — produces a
# plan + branch + initial scaffolding. Writes evidence.* on close. If
# the polecat detects a critical flag (e.g. issue blocked, unclear,
# duplicates an open PR, infeasible scope), it sets
# metadata.gc.halt_chain = true with a description; the gate step
# below short-circuits.

[[steps]]
id = "gate-after-pr-start"
title = "Gate: critical flags on pr-start?"
needs = ["pr-start"]
description = """
Read pr-start's evidence + metadata.gc.halt_chain.

If halt_chain is set OR critical findings present:
  - Set metadata.summary_for_human on the ROOT bead describing the flag
    + the recommended pivot (e.g. "pivot to issue #X instead")
  - Close THIS step with status=closed + close_reason citing the halt
  - Do NOT proceed to ship. The loop-close handler picks up the root
    bead close downstream and the PL surfaces the halt to slack.

Otherwise, close cleanly and proceed.
"""

[[steps]]
id = "ship"
title = "Pre-push gate — run mol-pr-ship checks"
needs = ["gate-after-pr-start"]
# Reuses mol-pr-ship step body. Produces a green/red verdict.

[[steps]]
id = "gate-after-ship"
title = "Gate: major concerns on ship?"
needs = ["ship"]
description = """
Read ship's evidence.reviewer_verdict.

If verdict != "pass":
  - Set metadata.summary_for_human on ROOT describing the major concern
  - Close this step, halt the chain
  - PL surfaces via loop-close

Otherwise close cleanly and proceed.
"""

[[steps]]
id = "open-pr"
title = "Push fork + open PR upstream"
needs = ["gate-after-ship"]
description = """
If vars.skip_open_pr == "true": close this step with notes
"skip_open_pr=true; branch ready at <sha> for human-driven open" and
exit cleanly.

Otherwise:
  - git push <fork-remote> <branch>
  - gh pr create --repo gastownhall/gascity --title <...> --body <...>
  - Write evidence on close:
    - evidence.artifact_path = "github-pr:gastownhall/gascity/<pr-num>"
    - evidence.reviewer_verdict = "pass"
    - evidence.reviewer_agent = "<this polecat session>"
  - Set metadata.summary_for_human on ROOT:
    "Opened PR #<N>: <title>. URL: <html_url>. CI status: <waiting|pending>."

If push 403s (per memory reference_maintainer_can_modify_misleading):
  halt the chain, set summary_for_human citing the supersede-via-our-
  fork recovery path.
"""

[[steps]]
id = "drain"
title = "Signal completion"
needs = ["open-pr"]
description = """
gc runtime drain-ack
"""
```

## Halt-and-surface mechanics

At each gate step, the polecat decides whether to halt or proceed
based on the prior step's evidence. When halting:

1. Polecat writes a clear `summary_for_human` on the ROOT bead (the
   one with originating_slack.* set), explaining the flag and
   proposing next moves.
2. Polecat closes the gate step (and skips downstream steps).
3. When the molecule completes (root bead closes), the loop-close
   handler (per dr-1lf4h3 Phase 2) nudges the PL, which composes a
   slack reply with the halt context + proposed pivots.
4. Stephanie reads the slack thread, decides direction, sends a new
   slack message — that's a new top-level ask that the PL routes
   into a new sling (e.g. "pivot to issue #X" → mol-pr-from-issue
   on the new issue).

## Variables surface

Default behavior: no flags except the ones the polecat detects. Use:

- `vars.issue` (required) — the upstream issue number
- `vars.skip_open_pr` (default false) — for cases where you want the
  branch ready but not opened yet (e.g. you'll open it manually)

Future variables to consider but NOT in v1:

- `vars.allow_skip_review` — bypass review step. Anti-pattern; not
  adding.
- `vars.target_repo` — non-gastownhall/gascity. Add when needed.
- `vars.draft` — open as draft. Add when needed.

## Out of scope

- Triage itself — that's mol-pr-triage; runs before this chain
- Stephanie's pick step — manual slack reply, not part of this formula
- Cross-repo work (gastownhall/gascity-packs) — that's its own pipeline
- Loop-close PL-mediation — tracked in dr-1lf4h3 Phase 2 rearch
- Human-gate surfacing for non-flag pauses — tracked in dr-xbs45x
- Sibling formulas in the dispatch table (mol-pr-blast-radius,
  mol-adopt-pr) — those are independent shapes

## Acceptance

- [ ] `mol-pr-from-issue.formula.toml` authored, follows
  `mol-adopt-pr` lifecycle conventions (steps + gates + drain)
- [ ] Reuses mol-pr-start, mol-pr-ship step bodies by copy-with-prune
  (NOT by include); per memory feedback_formula_choice_mol_do_work_vs_scoped
  narrow formulas over coupling via flags
- [ ] gascity-maintenance-pl dispatch table grows one row:
  `"author + ship + open PR for issue N"` → `mol-pr-from-issue`
- [ ] Each step writes `evidence.*` on close per gpk-24c contract
- [ ] Gate steps write `summary_for_human` on the root bead when
  halting
- [ ] Validation: pick a simple-to-author issue from a triage queue,
  sling mol-pr-from-issue, confirm the chain ends with an opened
  PR OR a halt-with-surface (depending on the issue's complexity)
- [ ] PR opened against gastownhall/gascity-packs:main referencing
  dr-i06w09 + dr-1lf4h3

## Dependencies

- gpk-3bi (summary_for_human contract on PR-pipeline formulas) — soft

title: [dr-1lf4h3-followup] Add evidence.* metadata writes to PR-pipeline formula close protocols
description:
# Add evidence.* metadata writes to PR-pipeline formula close protocols

## Why

During dr-1lf4h3 end-to-end validation (PR 1969 merge via mol-adopt-pr,
2026-05-11), the polecat closed gc-ja2x with a populated `close_reason`
string but did NOT write structured `evidence.*` metadata fields. Today
this is harmless because no gascity close-gate rule enforces them.

It IS a latent bug:

- `close_reason` is a free-text string, not machine-readable. The
  loop-close handler had to fall back to displaying it; future
  automation (pattern miner, retrospective tooling, audit reports)
  can't filter on verdict / commit / reviewer fields.
- The codeprobe rig has a parallel `codeprobe-drain-without-commit-guard`
  close-gate rule that enforces `evidence.artifact_path` /
  `evidence.reviewer_verdict` / `evidence.reviewer_agent` on close. If
  we ever turn on the same enforcement for gascity (which dr-1lf4h3
  acceptance lists as a deferred-but-planned step), every PR-pipeline
  close would get reopened until the formulas write the fields.
- The evidence pattern is the city's standard structured-close
  convention (see `feedback_codeprobe_worker_drain_without_commit`).

## Concrete missing fields on gc-ja2x

Reviewing the close_reason on gc-ja2x:

> "PR #1969 merged via mol-adopt-pr Path A squash. Decision: approve (0
> findings). Merge commit: 9f954698c456f36b90aea14f5d8bbf9f498f2e44.
> Synthesis: https://github.com/gastownhall/gascity/pull/1969#issuecomment-4423316614"

All the components exist; they just need to be structured:

```bash
bd update {{issue}} \
  --set-metadata "evidence.artifact_path=git:9f954698c456f36b90aea14f5d8bbf9f498f2e44" \
  --set-metadata "evidence.reviewer_verdict=pass" \
  --set-metadata "evidence.reviewer_agent=polecat-gc-164090"
```

(For mol-pr-review where the work-product is a scorecard not a merge,
`evidence.artifact_path` should reference the synthesis path or run_dir;
for mol-pr-blast-radius, the report path; etc.)

## Files to touch

Same set as gpk-3bi:

- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-adopt-pr.formula.toml`
  (PRIMARY — finalize / Path A close)
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-review.formula.toml`
  (close after scorecard write)
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-blast-radius.formula.toml`
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-ship.formula.toml`
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-triage.formula.toml`
  (if exists)
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-start.formula.toml`
  (if exists)

## Implementation

For each formula, in the same close-the-bead step where gpk-3bi adds
the `summary_for_human` block, ALSO add an `evidence.*` write
immediately before the `bd update --status=closed` call. The set is:

```bash
bd update {{issue}} \
  --set-metadata "evidence.artifact_path=<canonical artifact pointer for this formula>" \
  --set-metadata "evidence.reviewer_verdict=<pass|fail|pass-with-caveats|request_changes>" \
  --set-metadata "evidence.reviewer_agent=<your-session-name>"
```

Per-formula `evidence.artifact_path` shape:

| Formula | artifact_path |
|---|---|
| mol-adopt-pr (merge path) | `git:<merge-commit-sha>` |
| mol-adopt-pr (request-changes / decline path) | `gh-comment:<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<pr>#<comment-id>` |
| mol-pr-review | `path:<run_dir>/outputs/synthesis.md` |
| mol-pr-blast-radius | `path:<run_dir>/outputs/report.md` |
| mol-pr-ship | `git:<head-sha-at-push>` (pre-merge if just the gate) or `git:<merge-commit-sha>` (if also merged) |

Keep `close_reason` populated as well — it's the human-readable
narrative. The `evidence.*` metadata is the machine-readable index.

## Out of scope

- Turning on the close-gate rule for gascity. dr-1lf4h3 lists that
  separately as deferred-but-planned. Do not add the close-gate rule
  in this bead; the rule should land AFTER all formulas write the
  fields, with `enabled_since` set to the post-PR timestamp.
- mol-do-work — city-level, already has the close protocol but
  doesn't write evidence.* either. File a separate dr-* if we want
  city-level coverage. Most callers of mol-do-work read their rig's
  CLAUDE.md and pick up evidence requirements from there, so the
  pack-level fix is the higher-leverage one.
- mol-pr-ci-diagnose / mol-pr-merge-only — those are being authored
  in gpk-4id. Mention the evidence.* requirement in that bead's
  acceptance OR file a follow-up after that PR lands. (Authoring them
  with evidence.* writes from the start is preferable.)

## Acceptance

- [ ] Each PR-pipeline formula's close step writes the three
  `evidence.*` fields appropriate for its work-product type
- [ ] Validation: one formula cooked end-to-end on a real PR, evidence
  fields land in metadata structurally (verify with
  `bd show <id> --json | jq '.[0].metadata."evidence.artifact_path"'`)
- [ ] PR opened against gastownhall/gascity-packs:main, body references
  dr-1lf4h3 and this gpk-* bead
- [ ] gpk-4id (mol-pr-ci-diagnose / mol-pr-merge-only) authoring is
  notified to include evidence.* in its formulas from the start
  (cross-reference in PR description; do not block on completing it)

## Reference

- gc-ja2x close_reason (the live example missing structured evidence)
- gpk-3bi (sibling work — summary_for_human contract — overlaps file
  edits; coordinate to avoid merge conflicts)
- dr-1lf4h3 (parent loop-close work, lists close-gate-reaper as
  deferred-but-planned)
- `feedback_codeprobe_worker_drain_without_commit.md` — same
  pattern in the codeprobe rig
- `feedback_codeprobe_validation_gate.md` — codeprobe close-gate rule
  is the canonical shape for the gascity rule we'll eventually add

title: [dr-1lf4h3-impl] Add summary_for_human close protocol to PR-review pack formulas
description:
# Add summary_for_human close protocol to PR-review pack formulas

## Cross-rig parent

City-level work tracked at `dr-1lf4h3`. Mayor authored the loop-close
handler (`bin/pl-loop-close`) + `gc order` already; this bead handles
the formula-side contract update for the gascity-packs PR-review pack.

## Why

When a PL slings a polecat in response to a Slack ask, it writes
`metadata.originating_slack.*` on the tracking bead. The loop-close
handler reads this on bead-close and posts a threaded reply to the
originating Slack thread. The reply body is read from
`metadata.summary_for_human`, which the WORKER must write before close.

The worker-side contract change is small: every PR-pipeline formula
needs a step-body addition telling the polecat to write
`summary_for_human` before close when the bead has originating_slack.*.

mol-do-work has already been updated (city-side, in
`/home/ds/gas-city/formulas/mol-do-work.toml`). This bead extends the
pattern to the four PR-pipeline formulas in
`/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/`.

## Files to touch

- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-adopt-pr.formula.toml`
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-review.formula.toml`
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-blast-radius.formula.toml`
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-pr-ship.formula.toml`

If `mol-pr-triage.formula.toml` and `mol-pr-start.formula.toml` also
exist (they're in the gascity-maintenance-PL dispatch table), update
those too.

## Implementation

For each formula, find the close-the-bead step (typically the last
implementation step, the one that writes `evidence.*` and runs
`bd update --status=closed`). Add an inline subsection BEFORE the
close. The exact wording must match the wording added to mol-do-work
so the contract is uniform across formulas. Copy from
`/home/ds/gas-city/formulas/mol-do-work.toml` step `do-work`, section
labeled "**3a. Loop-close summary**":

```
**Loop-close summary (REQUIRED when bead has `metadata.originating_slack.*`)**

Check whether this bead was slung in response to a Slack ask:

```bash
bd show {{issue}} --json | jq -r '.[0].metadata.originating_slack // empty'
```

If the output is non-empty, you MUST write `metadata.summary_for_human`
before closing. The PL loop-close handler (`bin/pl-loop-close`, gc
order `pl-loop-close`) reads this field and posts it as a threaded
reply to the originating Slack thread. Format: 1-3 plain sentences,
no markdown, no emoji. Say what the human asked for and what you
delivered (or why you couldn't):

```bash
bd update {{issue}} \
  --set-metadata "summary_for_human=Reviewed PR #1969. Verdict: pass-with-caveats — the CI fix is correct but one test assertion is too loose; recommended a follow-up. Full scorecard in evidence path."
```

If you omit this on a slack-originated bead, the handler posts a
degraded reply naming the gap.
```

Adapt the example to the formula's domain (e.g. mol-pr-review's
example should reference a review verdict; mol-pr-ship's should
reference the merge result). Keep the structural language identical.

## Out of scope (DO NOT touch)

- `mol-pr-ci-diagnose.formula.toml` and `mol-pr-merge-only.formula.toml`
  — those don't exist yet; gpk-4id is creating them. Add the
  summary_for_human section there as part of authoring (gpk-4id may
  already cover this; if not, file a follow-up).
- Workspace-wide formula updates beyond gascity-packs/pr-review/
  — out of scope.
- Close-gate-reaper rule enforcement — deferred at the city level too;
  the handler degrades gracefully when summary_for_human is missing.
- Editing PL briefs to write originating_slack.* — that's done already
  at the city level for gascity-maintenance-pl, and is brief-scoped
  (not formula-scoped).

## Acceptance

- [ ] Each touched formula has the loop-close-summary section added
  before its close step
- [ ] Section wording is structurally identical across all four+ formulas
  (only the example payload varies per formula domain)
- [ ] One formula validated by cooking it against a real PR with
  originating_slack.* metadata pre-set (you can fake this by setting
  the metadata manually on a test bead — see Validation below)
- [ ] PR opened against gastownhall/gascity-packs:main referencing
  dr-1lf4h3 in the body

## Validation (single formula end-to-end)

Pick mol-pr-review (smallest scope). Create a test bead with
originating_slack metadata pre-set:

```bash
gc bd --rig gascity create "[loop-close-test] mol-pr-review on PR 1953" \
  --description "Test bead for loop-close validation. Sling mol-pr-review against #1953." \
  --type task --priority p3

# Get the new bead ID, then:
bd update <bead-id> --metadata '{
  "originating_slack": {
    "channel_id": "C0B25SS12CD",
    "thread_ts": "1234.5678",
    "user_id": "U-test",
    "asked_at": "2026-05-11T20:00:00Z"
  },
  "originating_pl_session": "test-session",
  "originating_pl_agent": "validation-test",
  "loop_close": "manual"
}'
```

`loop_close: "manual"` prevents the handler from actually posting to
Slack during the test (`auto` would post for real). Sling, let the
polecat run, and verify that `metadata.summary_for_human` ends up
populated on close. If yes, contract works; flip loop_close to "auto"
for the real validation in dr-1lf4h3 acceptance.

## Reference files

- `/home/ds/gas-city/formulas/mol-do-work.toml` — canonical wording
  for the summary_for_human section (copy from step `do-work`)
- `/home/ds/gas-city/bin/pl-loop-close` — handler that reads
  summary_for_human (for understanding what shape the field needs)
- gascity gc-mq4d / dr-1lf4h3 — broader context

# Implement mol-pr-ci-diagnose + mol-pr-merge-only formulas (gc-mq4d)

## Cross-rig parent

Approved mayor decision recorded at **gascity gc-mq4d** (notes):
- Option A green-lit 2026-05-11
- Rationale: bitter-lesson + `feedback_formula_choice_mol_do_work_vs_scoped` — narrow contracts over skip-flag coupling
- Option B (extending mol-adopt-pr with `skip_review` / `skip_human_gate` booleans) was correctly flagged by codex review as drifty under default-coercion

Read gc-mq4d's full PL Mayor Recommendation note before starting:
```bash
gc bd --rig gascity show gc-mq4d
```

## Scope

Author two new narrow PR-side formulas in `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/`:

### Formula 1: `mol-pr-ci-diagnose.formula.toml`

- **Vars:** `pr` (required)
- **Steps:** intake (parse PR), checks (gh pr checks), classify (log-failed + classify failures), report (write summary to bead notes + verdict)
- **Shape:** 4 steps, no rebase, no review, no human-gate
- **Use case:** "why is CI failing on PR N?"

### Formula 2: `mol-pr-merge-only.formula.toml`

- **Vars:** `pr` (required), `skip_gemini` (default false, kept for compat with mol-adopt-pr)
- **Steps:** intake (same shape as mol-adopt-pr.intake — parse + checkout), rebase-check (same shape as mol-adopt-pr.rebase-check), finalize (Path-A finalize from mol-adopt-pr.finalize)
- **Shape:** 3 steps, no review step, no human-gate
- **Use case:** "rebase + merge PR N, skip review" (maintainer has already eyeballed it)

**Reuse strategy:** copy-with-prune from `mol-adopt-pr` step bodies. Do NOT parameterize mol-adopt-pr with skip flags — that was the rejected Option B.

## Implementation steps (from gc-mq4d approval)

1. Author `mol-pr-ci-diagnose.formula.toml` (~80-100 lines)
2. Author `mol-pr-merge-only.formula.toml` (~130-160 lines — copy of mol-adopt-pr steps minus review/human-gate)
3. Update `pack.toml` + `README` only if listings exist there (filesystem discovery may make this a no-op — check before editing)
4. **NOTE: city-level dispatch-table prompt edit (`/home/ds/gas-city/agents/gascity-maintenance-pl/prompt.template.md`) is OUT OF SCOPE for this bead.** Mayor will do that single-file edit after this PR lands. You do not have write access to /home/ds/gas-city/. Do not attempt step 4 from the gc-mq4d plan.
5. Cook both formulas against PR #1953 (gastownhall/gascity, recently merged — has CI history and clean rebase trail) for validation
6. Multi-model review on the diff (mayor will run `/review` once you push)
7. Push to fork
8. Open PR against gastownhall/gascity-packs:main, body must reference gc-mq4d for the upstream context

## Read these reference files first

- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/mol-adopt-pr.formula.toml` — canonical source for intake / rebase-check / finalize step bodies. Copy-with-prune; do not reference by include.
- `/home/ds/gascity-packs/pr-review/pack.toml` — confirm whether explicit listings exist or pack uses filesystem discovery
- Existing formula naming + frontmatter conventions in `gascity-packs/pr-review/formulas/`

## Acceptance

- [ ] Both formulas authored and pass `bd formula lint` (or whatever the pack-side lint command is)
- [ ] Cooked successfully against PR #1953 — bead notes contain expected verdict shape (mol-pr-ci-diagnose) and rebase-check + Path-A finalize trail (mol-pr-merge-only)
- [ ] Multi-model review run (Claude + Codex at minimum); push only if reviews greenlight
- [ ] PR opened against gastownhall/gascity-packs:main, body references gc-mq4d
- [ ] Evidence on close: PR URL, commit hash of fork-side push, paths of both new formulas

title: [epic] slack-pack: tier the pack — slack-mini + slack-channel + slack-full
description:
Carve the slack-pack into three tiers so consumers can adopt the smallest
surface that fits their use case, instead of opting in or out of all 156
files of the current full integration.

## Tier contracts (drives the extraction)

```
Tier 1 — slack-mini       ~12-18 files     "Talk to mayor from Slack"
Tier 2 — slack-channel    ~50-60 files     "Bind your team channel to your session graph"
Tier 3 — slack-full       ~156+ files      "Workspace-grade multi-rig orchestration surface" (current pack)
```

Each tier strictly subsumes the prior. Migration up is additive (drop in the
larger pack); migration down requires re-deriving state (identity overrides,
channel mappings) but is documented.

### Tier 1 — slack-mini

Use case: add a bot to your company Slack, `@mayor` it from any channel, get
threaded replies.

In:
- Slack Events API receiver, only `app_mention`
- Inbound bridge-mail path: mention → `gc mail send mayor`
- One outbound verb: `gc slack post-message` (workspace-token-authed)
- 4 env vars (bot token, signing secret, workspace_id, city_name)

Out: bindings, registries, per-session identity, non-mention channels,
modals, files, fanout, multi-rig.

Files: ~12-18.

### Tier 2 — slack-channel

Use case: team has a `#ops` channel, bind it to mayor (and optionally PL),
team conversations route to the bound sessions, sessions post back. Per-session
identity overrides so different agents post as different bot personas.

Tier 1 + adds:
- Channel binding registry (channel ↔ N sessions, on-disk JSON)
- Verbs: `bind-dm`, `bind-room`, `publish`, `publish-to-channel`,
  `reply-current`, `identity`, `react`, `handle-alias` (cross-channel
  address-by-handle)
- Identity registry (display name + avatar overrides)
- Non-mention channel message routing to bound sessions
- Basic inbound modal-button ack

Out: multi-rig, peer fanout, room launcher, channel-name patterns,
file uploads, double-handle dispatch.

Files: ~50-60.

### Tier 3 — slack-full (current pack)

Use case: workspace orchestrates a multi-rig session graph; channels map to
rigs map to session pools; peers fan out replies; cross-channel handles;
file uploads; room launcher (`@@<handle>` spawns a session); slash-command
verb intake.

Tier 2 + adds: rig binding, channel-name pattern resolver, room launcher
mode, handle aliases, peer fanout, file upload, double-handle dispatch,
import-app + sync-commands, status / retry-peer-fanout / map-rig
diagnostics.

Files: ~156. Already lives at gastownhall/gascity-packs draft PR #8 once
that lands.

## Why this exists

Question from a reviewer on PR #8: "why does plugging in slack take 156
files?" The honest answer is "Tier 3 covers a lot of surface: webhooks,
modals, file uploads, peer fanout, multi-rig, room launcher; ~50% is
tests + pack-command boilerplate (3 files per verb is gc convention)."
But the question reveals a real adoption-friction problem: nobody trying
gc for the first time wants to opt into all 156 files just to wire up
"@mayor in Slack." Tiering is the answer.

## Out of scope

- Replacing tmux. Slack is a messaging surface above the agent layer;
  tmux is the runtime control plane below it. Different layers, no
  competition.
- Discord pack tiering. Discord is mostly Python today and already
  smaller than slack-full; tier this only after slack-mini lands and
  contributors validate the kernel pattern is worth replicating.

## Children

- .1 design memo: tier contracts + migration paths + import compatibility
- .2 slack-mini pack: extract Tier 1 from gascity-packs/slack-pack/ snapshot
- .3 slack-mini docs: install-in-3-minutes ergonomics, Slack manifest, README
- .4 slack-channel pack: build Tier 2 on top of Tier 1 kernel (NOT from
  Tier 3) — designed-fresh rather than carved-down
- .5 retitle existing slack-pack → slack-full + cross-link to .2/.4 +
  add migration-up note

WORK LOCATION: All children operate in /home/ds/gascity-packs (cross-tree
from polecat's gascity worktree, same pattern as gc-ejp.* and gc-px8.*).
Cut a feature branch off main (or off feat/import-slack-pack once that
merges) for each tier as a separate worktree. Do NOT modify Stephanie's
existing branches in gascity-packs.

Per memory feedback_no_remote_ops.md: workers do NOT push or open PRs.
Final hand-off includes branch + PR_DRAFT.md per tier; Stephanie pushes
+ opens PRs.

title: Surface report path and exit
description:
Display the report path and recommended pick on stdout for the maintenance
PL to relay back to Slack:

```bash
ROOT_ID=$(bd mol current --json | jq -r '.molecule_id')
NOTES=$(bd show "$ROOT_ID" --json | jq -r '.notes // ""')
TRIAGE_PATH=$(echo "$NOTES" | grep -m1 '^triage_path:' | sed 's/^triage_path: //')
TIER1=$(echo "$NOTES" | grep -m1 '^tier1:' | sed 's/^tier1: //')
TIER2=$(echo "$NOTES" | grep -m1 '^tier2:' | sed 's/^tier2: //')
TIER3=$(echo "$NOTES" | grep -m1 '^tier3:' | sed 's/^tier3: //')
TIER4=$(echo "$NOTES" | grep -m1 '^tier4:' | sed 's/^tier4: //')
RECOMMENDED=$(echo "$NOTES" | grep -m1 '^recommended:' | sed 's/^recommended: //')

cat <<EOF
[pr-triage] Triage report ready
[pr-triage]   Path: $TRIAGE_PATH
[pr-triage]   Counts: T1=$TIER1 T2=$TIER2 T3=$TIER3 T4=$TIER4
[pr-triage]   Recommended next pick: #$RECOMMENDED
[pr-triage] Next: human reviews report, picks an issue, dispatches mol-pr-start.
EOF

bd update "$ROOT_ID" --notes "status: complete"
bd close "$ROOT_ID" --reason "triage report written: $TRIAGE_PATH"
```

**Exit criteria:** Report path printed, root bead closed with status:complete.

title: Write the structured triage report
description:
Produce the markdown report at the path recorded in step 1.

```bash
ROOT_ID=$(bd mol current --json | jq -r '.molecule_id')
TRIAGE_PATH=$(bd show "$ROOT_ID" --json | jq -r '.notes // ""' | grep -m1 '^triage_path:' | sed 's/^triage_path: //')
```

Report structure:

```markdown
# Gas City triage — <ISO timestamp>

## Inputs

- Category filter: <category>
- Limit per tier: <limit>
- Open issues evaluated: <issue_count>
- Our open PRs: <our_pr_count>
- All open PRs: <all_pr_count>

## Counts by tier

- Tier 1 (GRAB NOW): <n>
- Tier 2 (GOOD CANDIDATE): <n>
- Tier 3 (INVESTIGATE FIRST): <n>
- Tier 4 (SKIP): <n>

## Tier 1 — GRAB NOW

For each issue:
### #<N> <title>
- Labels: <comma-separated>
- Gates: [DD AU MA PD]   ← all `.`
- Rationale: <one sentence on why this is grabbable now>
- Suggested dispatch: `mol-pr-start --var issue=<N>`

(repeat per Tier 1 issue, sorted by priority then recency)

## Tier 2 — GOOD CANDIDATE
(same shape as Tier 1)

## Tier 3 — INVESTIGATE FIRST
For each issue:
### #<N> <title>
- Labels: <...>
- Gates: [DD AU MA PD]   ← any `!`
- Demotion reason: <which gate fired and why>
- Suggested next step: read the relevant design doc / ask author / etc.

## Tier 4 — SKIP
For each issue:
### #<N> <title>
- Skip reason: assigned to <user> | covered by PR #<M> | label:wontfix | etc.

## Recommended next pick

The single best Tier 1 (or Tier 2 if no Tier 1) candidate, with:
- Issue # and title
- Why it's the best pick (priority, scope, no gates, no competing PR)
- The exact dispatch command:
  `gc-sling polecat <new-bead-id> --on mol-pr-start --var issue=<N>`

If no Tier 1 OR Tier 2 candidates exist, recommend a Tier 3 to investigate
or note that the queue is empty and triage should wait for new issues.
```

After writing the file:

```bash
TIER1=$(jq '[.[] | select(.tier==1)] | length' /tmp/triage-classified.json)
TIER2=$(jq '[.[] | select(.tier==2)] | length' /tmp/triage-classified.json)
TIER3=$(jq '[.[] | select(.tier==3)] | length' /tmp/triage-classified.json)
TIER4=$(jq '[.[] | select(.tier==4)] | length' /tmp/triage-classified.json)
RECOMMENDED=$(jq -r '[.[] | select(.tier <= 2)] | sort_by(.tier, -.priority_score) | .[0].number // "none"' /tmp/triage-classified.json)

bd update "$ROOT_ID" --notes "triage_path: $TRIAGE_PATH
tier1: $TIER1
tier2: $TIER2
tier3: $TIER3
tier4: $TIER4
recommended: $RECOMMENDED
status: report_written"
```

**Exit criteria:** Markdown report written; bead notes record path + tier counts + recommended pick.

title: mol-pr-triage
description:
PR triage workflow — scan open issues on the upstream repo, classify by
contributor-actionability, cross-reference with open PRs, and produce a
ranked work-queue report.

Mirrors the contributor-shape output of the local `/gascity-triage` skill,
but runs inside a polecat session so the maintenance PL can dispatch it
on demand. The deliverable is a markdown report at
`.gc/pr-pipeline/triage/<timestamp>.md` with Tier 1-4 classifications,
competing-PR detection, maintainer-decision-gate fires, and a recommended
next pick. **No code is written. No labels/comments are applied.** The
human caller reviews the report and decides which issue to dispatch via
`mol-pr-start`, which incoming PR to review via `mol-adopt-pr`, etc.

The molecule root bead IS the control bead — run state is recorded in its
notes via `bd update <root-id> --notes "<key>: <value>"`.

## Contract

1. Caller slings with optional `--var limit=<N>` (default 10) and
   `--var category=<filter>` (default "all"; accepts "p0", "p1", "p2",
   "good-first-issue", "all", or any single kind/priority label name)
2. Agent works through 5 sequential steps
3. Triage report written to `.gc/pr-pipeline/triage/<timestamp>.md`
4. Root-bead notes record `triage_path:` + tier counts + recommended pick
5. Agent exits — no implementation, labeling, or PR creation happens here

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Repo is not a git checkout | Record cwd, close bead |
| Repo has no remote / not gascity | Record error, close bead |
| `gh` not authenticated | Record error, close bead |
| No open issues match category | Write empty-queue report, close bead with note |

title: Pull open issues, our PRs, and all open PRs
description:
Three lists feed the classification:

```bash
ROOT_ID=$(bd mol current --json | jq -r '.molecule_id')
TS=$(grep -A1 'triage_path:' <(bd show "$ROOT_ID") | head -1 | sed 's|.*triage/||;s|\.md$||')
LIMIT=$(bd show "$ROOT_ID" --json | jq -r '.notes // "" | capture("limit: (?<v>\\d+)").v // "10"')
CATEGORY=$(bd show "$ROOT_ID" --json | jq -r '.notes // "" | capture("category: (?<v>\\S+)").v // "all"')
```

**1. Open issues (filtered by category):**
```bash
SEARCH="state:open repo:gastownhall/gascity"
case "$CATEGORY" in
    p0|p1|p2)        SEARCH="$SEARCH label:priority/$CATEGORY" ;;
    good-first-issue) SEARCH="$SEARCH label:good-first-issue" ;;
    all)             ;;
    *)               SEARCH="$SEARCH label:$CATEGORY" ;;
esac
gh search issues "$SEARCH" --limit 200 \
    --json number,title,labels,assignees,createdAt,updatedAt,body \
    > /tmp/triage-issues.json
```

**2. Our open PRs (sjarmak-authored) — extract issue refs from titles + bodies:**
```bash
gh pr list --repo gastownhall/gascity --author sjarmak --state open \
    --json number,title,body \
    > /tmp/triage-our-prs.json
```

**3. All open PRs — for competing-PR detection:**
```bash
gh pr list --repo gastownhall/gascity --state open \
    --json number,title,body,author \
    > /tmp/triage-all-prs.json
```

Record counts:
```bash
ISSUE_COUNT=$(jq 'length' /tmp/triage-issues.json)
OUR_PR_COUNT=$(jq 'length' /tmp/triage-our-prs.json)
ALL_PR_COUNT=$(jq 'length' /tmp/triage-all-prs.json)
bd update "$ROOT_ID" --notes "issue_count: $ISSUE_COUNT
our_pr_count: $OUR_PR_COUNT
all_pr_count: $ALL_PR_COUNT"
```

**Exit criteria:** Three JSON files written to /tmp; counts recorded in bead notes.

title: Classify each issue into Tier 1-4
description:
Apply the 4-tier classification to every issue. The agent uses semantic
judgment over the issue body, comments, labels, and PR cross-references.
The four tiers:

- **Tier 1 (GRAB NOW)** — Unassigned, clear bug, scope is bounded, no
  competing PR, no maintainer-decision gate fires, milestone matches a
  shippable target.
- **Tier 2 (GOOD CANDIDATE)** — Solid fix opportunity but needs a small
  amount of investigation (which file, which test, which sibling pattern).
- **Tier 3 (INVESTIGATE FIRST)** — Scope unclear, OR a maintainer-decision
  gate fires (design conflict, author uncertainty, maintainer ambivalence,
  product question disguised as bug).
- **Tier 4 (SKIP)** — Assigned to a non-self maintainer, OR an open PR
  already covers it, OR labelled won't-fix / on-hold / post-release.

**Competing-PR detection (CRITICAL — three methods, ANY hit demotes to Tier 4):**
1. Search by issue number: `gh pr list --repo gastownhall/gascity --search '<num>' --state open`
2. Search by issue title keywords: `gh pr list ... --search '<key phrases>' --state open`
3. Issue timeline: `gh api repos/gastownhall/gascity/issues/<num>/timeline --paginate` and
   look for `cross-referenced` events with `pull_request` source.

**Maintainer-decision gates (any fire demotes to Tier 3):**
- **DD (design-doc conflict):** grep `engdocs/design/*.md` for issue symbols; if an
  Accepted/Implementing doc contradicts the issue's proposed direction, fire.
- **AU (author uncertainty):** issue body ends with '?' or contains 'is this a bug or',
  'intended behavior', 'or at least', or proposes 2+ alternatives → fire.
- **MA (maintainer ambivalence):** a COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER comment with
  'great feedback' / 'good point' / 'we should' but no directive and no linked PR → fire.
- **PD (product decision):** Expected/Actual sections argue product opinions
  ('should be light', 'feels counter-intuitive') rather than pointing at a defect → fire.

For every Tier 1 and Tier 2 candidate, emit a `Gates: [DD AU MA PD]` line
where each letter is `.` (clear) or `!` (fire). Any `!` demotes the issue
to Tier 3 with the gate name in the demotion note.

The classification produces an in-memory list (or a temp JSON file) of:
```
{ "number": <N>, "title": "...", "tier": 1-4, "gates": "....",
  "rationale": "...", "competing_pr": <N or null>, "labels": [...],
  "assignee": "..." }
```

Cap the report at LIMIT issues per tier, sorted within each tier by:
- Tier 1: priority label > recency
- Tier 2: priority label > recency
- Tier 3: gate severity (DD > MA > AU > PD) > recency
- Tier 4: covering PR # ascending

Save the classified list to /tmp/triage-classified.json.

**Exit criteria:** /tmp/triage-classified.json contains one entry per
evaluated issue with tier, gates, and rationale fields populated.

title: Establish working state and verify repo
description:
**1. Find the root bead and confirm we're in the upstream gascity tree:**
```bash
ROOT_ID=$(bd mol current --json | jq -r '.molecule_id')
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null) || {
    echo "Not in a git repository — cannot triage."
    bd close "$ROOT_ID" --reason "not a git checkout"
    exit 1
}
cd "$REPO_ROOT"
REPO=$(gh repo view --json nameWithOwner -q .nameWithOwner)

if [ "$REPO" != "gastownhall/gascity" ]; then
    echo "Triage formula is gastownhall/gascity-specific. Got: $REPO"
    bd update "$ROOT_ID" --notes "status: blocked
gate: wrong_repo
detail: $REPO"
    bd close "$ROOT_ID" --reason "wrong repo for triage"
    exit 0
fi
```

**2. Verify gh auth:**
```bash
gh auth status >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
    bd update "$ROOT_ID" --notes "status: blocked
gate: gh_not_authed"
    bd close "$ROOT_ID" --reason "gh not authenticated"
    exit 1
}
```

**3. Resolve vars and record initial state:**
```bash
LIMIT="{{limit}}"
[ -z "$LIMIT" ] && LIMIT=10
CATEGORY="{{category}}"
[ -z "$CATEGORY" ] && CATEGORY="all"
TS=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
TRIAGE_DIR=".gc/pr-pipeline/triage"
mkdir -p "$TRIAGE_DIR"
TRIAGE_PATH="$TRIAGE_DIR/$TS.md"

bd update "$ROOT_ID" --notes "category: $CATEGORY
limit: $LIMIT
repo: $REPO
triage_path: $TRIAGE_PATH
status: in_progress"
```

**Exit criteria:** In gastownhall/gascity repo root, gh auth ok, vars + paths recorded.
